markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 20-39 tweets from March 27 to April 3, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 27 12:00 PM ET to April 3, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

events.title: Elon Musk # tweets March 27 - April 3, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 27 12:00 PM ET to April 3, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders peg Elon Musk's X posts for March 27-April 3, 2026, in a tight cluster around 260-339, with 300-319 (11.5%), 260-279 (11%), and nearby ranges each hovering at 10-11% implied probabilities, capturing his signature high-volume social media cadence averaging 35-50 posts daily. Absent recent shifts in his activity—no major controversies, product launches, or political flare-ups in the past 30 days—these odds mirror long-term patterns fueled by Tesla updates, SpaceX milestones, xAI buzz, and cultural commentary that routinely drive 200-400 weekly totals. Competitive dynamics hinge on event volatility: surges from breaking news could push toward 340+, while focused ventures might dip below 260, though the distant timeline tempers conviction amid X's evolving algorithm and Musk's unpredictable priorities.

markets.question: Will Movement for a Better Hungary (Jobbik) win the most seats in the next Hungarian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Hungarian National Assembly (Országgyűlés) election. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Hungarian Parliament. If a named coalition dissolves, this market will resolve based on the seat total of the constituent party within that coalition that held the largest number of seats before the election.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Hungarian government, specifically the Hungarian Election Authority (Nemzeti Választási Iroda) (valasztas.hu).

events.title: Hungary Parliamentary Election Winner
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Hungary in early April 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Hungarian National Assembly (Országgyűlés) election. 

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party or coalition whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Hungarian Parliament. If a named coalition dissolves, this market will resolve based on the seat total of the constituent party within that coalition that held the largest number of seats before the election.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Hungarian government, specifically the Hungarian Election Authority (Nemzeti Választási Iroda) (valasztas.hu).

eventMetadata.context_description: Péter Magyar's TISZA party holds a commanding lead in trader consensus at 66.5% for Hungary's 2026 parliamentary election under its mixed single-member district and proportional representation system, fueled by recent polls from firms like Medián and Publicus showing TISZA ahead by 15-25 points over Fidesz-KDNP's 33.5%. This reflects Magyar's surge as a credible opposition challenger since defecting from Fidesz in early 2024, capitalizing on public discontent with Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's administration amid protests over a child abuse pardon scandal, frozen EU funds, and economic pressures. Fidesz faces fragmented opposition elsewhere but struggles with eroding support in swing districts; no snap election is scheduled, leaving 16 months for shifts via coalition negotiations or further scandals.

markets.question: Netanyahu out by March 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Benjamin Netanyahu ceases to be Prime Minister of Israel for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Benjamin Netanyahu and the government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Netanyahu out by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Benjamin Netanyahu announces that he will resign as Prime Minister of Israel, or otherwise steps down from/is removed from this position by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation or removal before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the State of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's right-wing coalition maintains a slim majority in the Knesset, surviving no-confidence votes last week amid mass protests over the Gaza war's protracted stalemate and hostage crisis. Tensions escalated with far-right and ultra-Orthodox allies during narrow passage of a military draft exemption bill for Haredi Jews, averting a potential government collapse. Polls show opposition parties, led by Yair Lapid and Benny Gantz, commanding double-digit leads, intensifying calls for a snap election though none is scheduled before October 2026 absent a coalition fracture. Traders eye upcoming budget votes, judicial reform pushes, and ceasefire talks as pivotal tests of stability.

markets.question: US x Iran ceasefire by March 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

events.title: US x Iran ceasefire by...?
events.description:

For the purposes of this market, an “official ceasefire agreement” requires clear public confirmation from both the United States government and the government of Iran that they have agreed to halt military hostilities against one another.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the United States and Iran, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the United States government and the government of Iran. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tensions between the US and Iran remain elevated but have de-escalated since Iran's October 1 missile attack on Israel—intercepted with US assistance—and Israel's October 26 airstrikes on Iranian military facilities, which Tehran downplayed as limited. Iranian leaders, including Supreme Leader Khamenei's advisor, signaled no further retaliation, while the Biden administration distanced itself from Israel's action and reiterated calls for restraint amid ongoing US strikes against Iran-backed Houthis disrupting Red Sea shipping. Absent direct US-Iran combat, a formal ceasefire appears improbable without major provocation; proxy conflicts, nuclear talks stalemate, and sanctions persist. The incoming Trump administration's foreign policy shift post-January 20 inauguration, alongside Gaza negotiations, could influence dynamics.

markets.question: Will Jordan win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain tops trader consensus at 16% implied probability for the 2026 FIFA World Cup following their dominant Euro 2024 victory, where young stars like Lamine Yamal and a flawless knockout run propelled them past England in the final, signaling sustained depth and tactical cohesion under Luis de la Fuente. England lingers close at 13% after that runner-up finish, bolstered by resilient defending and attacking threats like Harry Kane, while France (11%) maintains elite status via Nations League form and Olympic final appearance, and Argentina (10%) leverages back-to-back Copa América titles amid Lionel Messi's enduring influence. Brazil sits at 9% despite Copa struggles, reflecting qualification uncertainties in CONMEBOL's tight table. The bunched top reflects the tournament's two-year horizon, ongoing UEFA/AFC/CAF qualifiers, aging stars, emerging talents, and the expanded 48-team format heightening upset potential across a deep global field.

markets.question: Will Baidu have the best AI model at the end of March 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company which owns the model which has the highest arena score based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on March 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Arena Score" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

If two models are tied for the highest arena score at this market's check time, resolution will be based on whichever company's name, as it is described in this market group, comes first in alphabetical order (e.g., if both were tied, "Google" would resolve to "Yes", and "xAI" would resolve to "No")

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anthropic's Claude 3 Opus release on March 4 has driven near-unanimous trader consensus at 98.9% implied probability for possessing the top AI model by month's end, topping benchmarks like LMSYS Chatbot Arena, MMLU, and GPQA in reasoning, coding, and multimodal tasks, surpassing OpenAI's GPT-4 and Google's Gemini. This large language model demonstrated superior capabilities in real-world evaluations, shifting competitive positioning decisively amid a crowded field. While regulatory scrutiny on AI safety looms, no credible challengers have emerged; scenarios like a late OpenAI o1 preview or benchmark revisions could theoretically disrupt, but traders see minimal risk given timelines and historical launch patterns. Key resolution hinges on end-of-March leaderboard standings.

markets.question: Will the Iranian regime fall by March 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.description:

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices "No" at 99.2% for the Iranian regime falling by March 31, driven by the absence of any major internal challenges or uprisings in the past 30 days. Sporadic protests persist amid economic sanctions and inflation, but the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) maintains tight security control, as shown in swift suppression of dissent and resilience during recent Israel-Iran exchanges—including Iran's October 1 missile strikes and Israel's limited responses. Supreme Leader Khamenei's authority remains unchallenged, with no credible reports of health crises, defections, or factional splits. While low-probability scenarios like a sudden leadership vacuum, widespread military mutiny, or foreign invasion could theoretically shift odds, no catalysts have emerged to suggest regime collapse within the timeline.

markets.question: US forces enter Iran by March 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if active US military personnel physically enter Iran at any point by the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Military special operation forces will qualify, however intelligence operatives will not count.

US military personnel must physically enter the terrestrial territory of Iran to qualify. Entering Iran's maritime or aerial territory will not count.

Military contractors, military advisors, or high-ranking US service members entering Iran for diplomatic purposes (and their accompanying entourage) will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: US maintains defensive posture amid Israel-Iran tensions, with no US ground forces entering Iran despite recent escalations. Israel's October 26 airstrikes targeted Iranian missile production sites near Tehran, retaliating for Iran's October 1 barrage; Washington provided intelligence assistance and THAAD defenses for Israel but explicitly ruled out offensive strikes. President Biden urged de-escalation to avoid regional war, repositioning naval assets and troops for deterrence against Iranian proxies like Hezbollah. No announcements signal invasion plans, prioritizing diplomacy and sanctions. Traders watch for Iranian retaliation signals or post-election shifts after November 5, which could alter escalation risks.

markets.question: Texas Longhorns vs. Purdue Boilermakers
markets.description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for March 26 at 12:00 AM ET:

If the Texas Longhorns win, the market will resolve to "Texas Longhorns".

If the Purdue Boilermakers win, the market will resolve to "Purdue Boilermakers".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.title: Texas Longhorns vs. Purdue Boilermakers
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between Texas Longhorns and Purdue Boilermakers on March 26 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Purdue Boilermakers' elite frontcourt size and defensive efficiency anchor their 73.5% implied probability as heavy favorites over Texas Longhorns in this NCAA Tournament first-round clash. The No. 5-seeded Boilermakers (27-8) boast a stifling No. 3-ranked adjusted defensive rating, allowing just 63.8 points per game, led by Trey Kaufman-Renn's 16.7 PPG and rim protection that neutralizes Texas' perimeter-heavy attack. Purdue enters hot with four straight wins, including Big Ten tourney semis, while No. 4 Texas (25-8) shows road vulnerabilities (4-5 away) and faces rest disadvantage after a tight Big 12 final loss. No key Purdue injuries per official reports; Texas' Chaz Lanier fully cleared despite earlier ankle concern. Historical matchup edges favor Purdue's physicality, mirroring trader consensus on Boilermakers' 78% win rate as chalk.

markets.question: Spread: Pistons (-4.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 26 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Pistons" if the Pistons win the game by 5 or more points.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Pelicans". If the game ends in a tie, this market will resolve to "Pelicans".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 26 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Pelicans win, the market will resolve to "Pelicans".

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Pistons' 67.5% implied probability reflects their strong home-court advantage at Little Caesars Arena and recent surge, winning three of their last four games behind Cade Cunningham's efficient scoring and Tim Hardaway Jr.'s hot shooting. The New Orleans Pelicans, mired in a 2-5 slump, face dire injury challenges: Zion Williamson is sidelined with a hamstring strain, Brandon Ingram remains out with ankle issues, and Dejounte Murray is questionable with back spasms per the latest injury report. Matchup dynamics favor Detroit's improved defense against New Orleans' depleted frontcourt, aligning trader consensus with historical Pistons dominance in similar spots against banged-up opponents.

markets.question: Will Trump visit China by April 30?
markets.description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits China by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.description:

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President-elect Donald Trump's potential visit to China remains unannounced amid heightened US-China tensions driven by his proposed 60% tariffs on Chinese imports, reiterated in recent interviews. Following his November 2024 election victory, Chinese President Xi Jinping sent a congratulatory letter emphasizing stable bilateral relations, but no summit invitations or travel plans have emerged from transition discussions. Trump's team focuses on cabinet nominations and domestic policy, with inauguration on January 20, 2025, likely delaying foreign trips—historical precedents show new presidents prioritizing allies like Canada or Mexico first. Upcoming events, such as possible APEC or G20 engagements post-inauguration, could signal diplomacy, though tariff rhetoric dominates trader assessments of near-term travel odds.

markets.question: Will Byron Donalds win the 2028 Republican presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as the narrow favorite at 49% for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, propelled by President-elect Donald Trump's November 14 announcement naming him HHS Secretary, which amplifies his visibility after endorsing Trump and aligning with administration priorities on health policy. J.D. Vance trails closely at 36.6% as vice president-elect, benefiting from incumbency advantages, historical VP-to-nominee paths, and expected Trump endorsement influence in primaries. Marco Rubio holds third at 22.1%, buoyed by his Senate leadership and Florida battleground appeal. Recent cabinet nominations reflect Trump's inner circle dynamics shaping early speculation, with Senate confirmation hearings as the next potential market mover amid an open field before 2028 primaries.

eventMetadata.context_description: Houston's elite defense, ranked No. 1 nationally in adjusted efficiency, anchors the Cougars' 56.5% implied probability as slight favorites against Illinois in the NCAA Sweet 16. The top seed has suffocated recent opponents, holding Texas A&M to 37% shooting in a 100-95 overtime win after dominating Longwood. Illinois, riding momentum from comeback victories over Morehead State and Iowa State, boasts a potent offense led by Terrence Shannon Jr. (23.2 PPG), but faces a tough matchup against Houston's physicality and rebounding edge (No. 4 nationally). No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, with Jamal Shead fully active; neutral-site dynamics in Boston favor Houston's deeper rotation and two-way play. Trader consensus reflects the Cougars' historical success in stifling high-scoring Big Ten attacks.

markets.question: Will Israel take military action in Lebanon on March 20, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Lebanese soil on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "military action" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Lebanese ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of Lebanon counts, including buffer zones.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land on Lebanese territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ground based ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israeli airstrikes intensified against Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon over the past week, including strikes on command centers and rocket launchers amid ongoing cross-border fire, anchoring trader caution following Israel's October 1 ground incursion to dismantle Hezbollah infrastructure near the border. The September 27 assassination of Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah triggered this escalation phase, with no major de-escalation despite U.S.-mediated ceasefire talks involving Qatar and France. Hezbollah retaliation rockets persist, though reduced, while Israeli troop deployments signal sustained operations. Traders eye upcoming UN Security Council sessions and potential diplomatic breakthroughs, as failure could prompt broader invasion or airstrikes deeper into Lebanon.

markets.question: Dota 2: Team Yandex vs Team Spirit (BO3) - ESL One Birmingham Playoffs
markets.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Upper bracket semifinal 1 match between Team Yandex and Team Spirit in the ESL One Birmingham Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 26 at 8:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Yandex" if Team Yandex win the match against Team Spirit.

This market will resolve to "Team Spirit" if Team Spirit win the match against Team Yandex.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Spirit holds a commanding edge over Team Yandex in this ESL One Birmingham Playoffs BO3 series, driven by their world #1 ranking, TI 2021 title pedigree, and dominant group stage run with 2-0 sweeps against top opponents. Yandex advanced via the open qualifiers and challengers bracket, showcasing upset potential with gritty wins over mid-table squads like Execration, but their roster lacks Spirit's synergy in the current patch 7.36c meta emphasizing early aggression and macro control. Stable lineups on both sides—no reported changes or visa issues—with Spirit's Collapse and Larl excelling on cores; traders eye draft advantage and map-one momentum as pivotal, alongside Yandex's underdog draft creativity for upset viability. Recent LAN adaptation remains key for the Russian matchup.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 220-239 tweets from March 20 to March 27, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from March 20 12:00 PM ET to March 27, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Elon Musk posting 260-279 tweets during March 20-27, 2026 (49.8% implied probability), with 280-299 close behind (26.5%), mirroring his established high-volume cadence on X averaging 35-45 daily posts. This positioning draws from verified recent patterns, including 2024 peaks exceeding 300 weekly amid election fervor, Tesla announcements, and viral cultural clashes, sustaining momentum into 2025 without notable dips. No fresh catalysts in the last 48 hours, but his role amplifying Trump administration narratives and SpaceX milestones keeps expectations elevated. Unpredictability looms from algorithm tweaks, personal downtime, or breaking news, yet historical skin-in-the-game data from traders reinforces this mid-200s to low-300s cluster as the frontrunner trajectory.

markets.question: Wild vs. Panthers
markets.description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for March 26 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Wild win, the market will resolve to "Wild".
If the Panthers win, the market will resolve to "Panthers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Florida Panthers' scorching 8-1-1 start and Sergei Bobrovsky's .935 save percentage anchor trader consensus favoring them at home against the Minnesota Wild, whose 4-5-2 record masks key injuries like Brock Faber's lower-body issue and a middling penalty kill. Recent Panthers momentum surged with a 4-1 rout of rival Tampa Bay, while Wild stumbled 3-2 at Dallas amid defensive breakdowns. Head-to-head, Florida holds a 6-2-2 edge in last 10 meetings, amplified by Amerant Bank Arena's fortress-like 7-game win streak. Wild's road woes (1-3-1) and power-play drought (14.3%) heighten upset risk, but markets weigh Panthers' Cup-champion depth as the edge.

markets.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $66,000 on March 26?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Detroit Pistons' 67.5% implied probability reflects their strong home-court advantage at Little Caesars Arena and recent surge, winning three of their last four games behind Cade Cunningham's efficient scoring and Tim Hardaway Jr.'s hot shooting. The New Orleans Pelicans, mired in a 2-5 slump, face dire injury challenges: Zion Williamson is sidelined with a hamstring strain, Brandon Ingram remains out with ankle issues, and Dejounte Murray is questionable with back spasms per the latest injury report. Matchup dynamics favor Detroit's improved defense against New Orleans' depleted frontcourt, aligning trader consensus with historical Pistons dominance in similar spots against banged-up opponents.
